Gardnerella Strain Metronidazole MICs

Hanna Berman

April 03, 2026

Purpose: To assess the minimum inhibitory concentration of metronidazole on Gardnerella isolates.

Protocol:

  • Streak strains onto BHI agar plates with 1% yeast extract and 0.5% dextrose
  • Inoculate colonies into 1mL NYC-III media and incubate for 24 hours at 37°C anaerobically (48 hours for strains C0084H9, C0093B3, C0101A1, UM35)
  • Measure OD600 of culture are dilute to 0.1 in PBS
    • If cultures OD600 is not greater than or equal to 0.1, back dilute 1:100 and incubate another 24 or 48 hours
    • Cultures with OD600 < 0.1 not used
  • Plate lawns (two technical replicates per culture) onto BHI with 1% yeast extract and 0.5% dextrose
  • Place a metronidazole MIC test strip into center of plate
  • Incubate at 37°C anaerobically for 48 hours
  • Observe MIC as lowest dose where growth is inhibited
  • Perform again for second biological replicate
library(tidyverse)
library(cowplot)
library(formattable)
library(kableExtra)
`%!in%` <- negate(`%in%`)

Short cuts

strains <- c("14018", "315-A", "C0011E4", "JCP7275", "C0084H9", "JCP8108", "JCP8017B", "C0040C2" , "UM35",  "JCP8066", "C0093B3", "AMD", "C0096A1", "C0102A2",  "UM224", "Gv5-1", "C0179B4", "C0056B5", "Gv101", "C0100B2", "C0101A1", "CMW7778B")

species <- c("Gardnerella vaginalis", "Gardnerella lacydonensis", "Gardnerella pickettii", "Gardnerella piotii", "Gardnerella leopoldii", "Gardnerella swidsinskii", "Gardnerella sp. 7", "Gardnerella greenwoodii", "Gardnerella sp. 10", "Gardnerella sp. 11", "Gardnerella sp. 12")

speciesAbbrevs <- c("G. vag.", "G. lac.", "G. pic.", "G. pio.", "G. leo.", "G. swi.", "7", "G. gre.", "10", "11", "12")

metroDoses <- c("0.016", "0.023", "0.032", "0.047", "0.064", "0.094", "0.125", "0.19", "0.25", "0.38", "0.50", "0.75", "1.0", "1.5", "2", "3", "4", "6", "8", "12", "16", "24", "32", "48", "64", "96", "128", "192", "256", ">256")

h48Strains <- c("C0084H9", "C0093B3", "C0101A1", "UM35") # strains with 48-hour incubation period

figureOut <- "../../experiments_figures"

Import data and add updated species names

strainIDs  <- read_csv("./data/strainIDs.csv") %>%
    mutate(Species=case_when(Species=="Gardnerella sp. 2"~"Gardnerella lacydonensis",
                           Species=="Gardnerella sp. 3"~"Gardnerella pickettii",
                           Species=="Gardnerella sp. 8"~"Gardnerella greenwoodii",
                           Species %!in% c("Gardnerella sp. 2", "Gardnerella sp. 3", "Gardnerella sp. 8")~Species))

strainODs <-  read_csv("./data/strainODs.csv")

strainMICs <- read_csv("./data/strainMICs.csv")

strainSpecies <- strainIDs %>%
  select(Strain, Species) %>%
  unique

Plot settings

theme_set(theme_bw())

speciesColor <- scale_color_manual(values=c("#CC79A7", "#D55E00", "#56B4E9", "#0072B2", "#E69F00", "#009E73", 
                                                 "darkgreen", "gold3", "darkred", "darkblue",
                                                 "mediumpurple4"))

speciesFill <- scale_fill_manual(values=c("#CC79A7", "#D55E00", "#56B4E9", "#0072B2", "#E69F00", "#009E73", 
                                                 "darkgreen", "gold3", "darkred", "darkblue",
                                                 "mediumpurple4"))

Growth QC by Optical Density

odDF <- strainODs %>%
  left_join(strainIDs) %>%
  with_groups(Strain, mutate, BioRepRank=dense_rank(BioRep)) %>%
  with_groups(c(Strain, BioRepRank), mutate, ODmeasure=as.character(dense_rank(ODdate))) %>%
  mutate(incubationPeriod=case_when(Strain %in% h48Strains~"48",
                                  !(Strain %in% h48Strains)~"24"))
xAxisColors0 <- odDF %>%
  select(Strain, incubationPeriod) %>%
  unique %>%
  arrange(Strain) %>%
  .$incubationPeriod
xAxisColors <- ifelse(xAxisColors0=="24", "black", "blue")

odDF %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=Strain, y=OD, color=ODmeasure)) +
  geom_point(size=3, alpha=0.5) +
  geom_hline(yintercept=0.1, color="gray", linetype=2) +
  scale_x_discrete(drop=FALSE) +
  #scale_shape_manual(values=c(3, 4)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45, hjust=1, color=xAxisColors)) +
  labs(x=NULL, y="OD600", shape="Biological Replicate")

Strain names listed in blue are in the 48-hour to stationary phase group. Points in teal indicate 1:100 back dilution in fresh NYC-III media from broth culture and subsequent incubation.

Metronidazole Minimum Inhibitory Concentrations

# format MIC data with strain and sample names
micDF <- strainMICs %>%
    left_join(strainIDs) %>%
    mutate(Sample=paste(Strain, BioRep, TechRep, sep=".")) %>%
    select(Sample, Strain, Species, Number, BioRep, TechRep, MIC, Notes)

#save formatted phenotype data
# micDF %>%
#   select(Sample, Strain, Species, MIC, Notes) %>%
#   write_csv("../formatted_phenotype_data/metro_mic.csv")


micDF1 <- micDF %>%
  filter(MIC != "hard to read",  # filter samples that could not be read
         MIC != "unable to read",
         MIC != "too streaky to read", 
         MIC != "Streaky") %>%
  replace_na(list(Notes="None")) %>%
  with_groups(Strain, mutate, BioRepRank=dense_rank(BioRep)) %>%
  mutate(MIC=factor(MIC, levels=metroDoses),
         Strain=factor(Strain, levels=strains),
         BioRepRank=as.character(BioRepRank),   
         ColoniesThroughout=str_detect(Notes, "colonies")) # create a true/false column for samples with colonies throughout

Figure 6: Metronidazole MICs

micPlot <- micDF1 %>%
  mutate(Strain=factor(Strain, levels = strains),
         Species=factor(Species, levels = species)) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x=Strain, y=MIC, shape=BioRepRank, color=ColoniesThroughout)) +
    geom_point(size=3, alpha=0.5) +
    scale_x_discrete(drop=FALSE) +
    scale_y_discrete(drop=FALSE) +
    scale_shape_manual(values=c(3, 4, 5, 6)) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("black", "blue")) +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45, hjust=1)) +
    labs(x=NULL, y="Metronidazole MIC", shape="Biological Replicate", color="Colonies Throughout")

speciesBar <- strainSpecies %>%
  mutate(Strain=factor(Strain, levels = strains),
         Species=factor(Species, levels = species),
         SpeciesLab=factor(Species, levels=species, labels=speciesAbbrevs)) %>%
  with_groups(Species, mutate, num=rank(Strain)) %>%
  mutate(SpeciesLab=case_when(num==1~SpeciesLab,
                              num!=1~"")) %>%
  ggplot() +
    geom_bar(aes(x=Strain, y=1, fill=Species), stat="identity", width=1.1) +
    geom_text(aes(x=Strain, y=0.5, label=SpeciesLab), color="white", hjust = 0.25, size=3) +
    speciesFill +
    theme_void() +
    theme(legend.position = "none")

plot_grid(speciesBar, micPlot, ncol=1, rel_heights = c(0.08, 1),  align = "v", axis = "lr")

#ggsave(file.path(figureOut, paste(Sys.Date(), "Figure6_metro_mic.png", sep="_")))

The MIC of most strains was >256. Results were reproduced with most strains. UM35 displayed variability among biological replicates. The MIC for biological replicate 1 was >256 and 16 for replicate 2. Two additional biological replicates yielded results between these values: MICs of 24, 64, 96.

Points that are labeled in blue are cases where there was no lawn past the MIC but there were colonies. These occurred in two isolates in Gardnerella Gardnerella lacydonensis. One of these isolates, C0084H9 displayed one biological replicate with this result and one with an MIC >256. The third G. lacydonensis isolate, JCP7275 had an MIC of >256.

Table 2: MIC Ranges and Susceptibility of Metronidazole

# Summary table
MICtable <- micDF1 %>%
  mutate(MIC = as.character(MIC),
         MICnum = str_extract(MIC, "[^>]+$") %>% as.numeric()) %>% # create a numeric column of MIC
  group_by(Strain, Species) %>%
  summarize(minMIC=min(MICnum), maxMIC=max(MICnum), CT=any(ColoniesThroughout==TRUE)) %>%
  mutate(`Metronidazole resistance`=case_when(minMIC>=32 & maxMIC>=32 & CT==FALSE ~ "+",
                             minMIC<32 & maxMIC<32 & CT==FALSE ~ "-",
                             minMIC<32 & maxMIC >= 32 ~ "variable",
                             CT==TRUE ~ "variable"),
         minMIC=as.character(minMIC),
         maxMIC=as.character(maxMIC),
         minMIC=paste0("[", minMIC, "]"),
         maxMIC=paste0("[", maxMIC, "]"),
         minMIC=case_when(minMIC=="[256]" ~ paste0(">", minMIC),
                          TRUE~minMIC),
         maxMIC=case_when(maxMIC=="[256]" ~ paste0(">", maxMIC),
                          TRUE~maxMIC),
         `MIC range`=case_when(minMIC!=maxMIC ~ paste(minMIC, maxMIC, sep=" - "),
                               minMIC==maxMIC ~ maxMIC),
         `Colonies`=case_when(CT==TRUE~"*",
                              CT==FALSE~"")) %>%
  select(Species, Strain, `MIC range`, `Metronidazole resistance`, Colonies) %>%
  kbl(caption="Table 2. Minimum inhibitory concentration and resistance of metronidazole") %>%
  kable_classic(full_width=TRUE, html_font = "Arial")

MICtable
Table 2. Minimum inhibitory concentration and resistance of metronidazole
Species Strain MIC range Metronidazole resistance Colonies
Gardnerella vaginalis 14018 [12] - [24]
Gardnerella vaginalis 315-A [32] - [48]
Gardnerella vaginalis C0011E4 >[256]
Gardnerella lacydonensis JCP7275 >[256]
Gardnerella lacydonensis C0084H9 [2] - >[256] variable
Gardnerella lacydonensis JCP8108 [16] - [24] variable
Gardnerella pickettii JCP8017B [16]
Gardnerella pickettii C0040C2 >[256]
Gardnerella piotii UM35 [16] - >[256] variable
Gardnerella piotii JCP8066 >[256]
Gardnerella piotii C0093B3 >[256]
Gardnerella leopoldii AMD >[256]
Gardnerella leopoldii C0096A1 >[256]
Gardnerella swidsinskii C0102A2 >[256]
Gardnerella swidsinskii UM224 >[256]
Gardnerella swidsinskii Gv5-1 >[256]
Gardnerella sp. 7 C0179B4 >[256]
Gardnerella greenwoodii C0056B5 >[256]
Gardnerella greenwoodii Gv101 >[256]
Gardnerella sp. 11 C0101A1 [48] - >[256]
Gardnerella sp. 12 CMW7778B [16] - [48] variable 
#save_kable(MICtable, file = file.path(figureOut, paste(Sys.Date(), "Table2_MICTable.png", sep="_")), zoom=6)

Missing Replicates

Aimed to have two biological replicates with two technical replicates each, but the following isolates have fewer that two biological replicates:

s1 <- micDF1 %>%
  with_groups(Strain, summarize, n=n_distinct(BioRep)) %>%
  filter(n<2) 
s1 %>%
  bind_rows(., list(Strain=setdiff(strainIDs$Strain, micDF1$Strain),n=0)) %>%
  dplyr::rename("n biological replicates"=n) %>%
  formattable
Strain n biological replicates
JCP8017B 1
C0096A1 1
C0100B2 0

Two strains, JCP8017B and C0096A1, have only 1 biological replicate and C0100B2 did not grow well enough to perform the experiment.
